Tests if multiplying `undefined` by a `Boolean` object results in `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the result of an arithmetic operation involving `undefined` and a `Boolean` object. The expression `undefined * new Boolean(true)` is expected to evaluate to `NaN` (Not-a-Number), and the `assert` function verifies that this is true. The use of `isNaN` confirms that the result of the multiplication is indeed `NaN`. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a test case to ensure that the JavaScript engine correctly handles this specific operation.
